channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest

item.title: [An RX Global Event] Infosecurity Europe

item.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
item.description: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'CIFSwitch' in the Linux kernel could allow attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions, abuse the kernel's key request m...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, tracked as ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
item.description: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to display fake OpenAI outage pages that direct users to download malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: California AG sues 23andMe over 2023 breach exposing health data
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, now Chrome Holding Co., over the company's failure to protect sensitive customer genetic and personal information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attackers Use LLM Agent for Post-Exploitation After Marimo CVE-2026-39987 Exploit
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed using a large language model (LLM) agent to conduct post-compromise actions after obtaining initial access following the exploitation of a publicly-accessible...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
item.description: The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a a variety of factors, but that could be changing....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: From $5 Attacks to Botnet-Powered Platforms: Inside the DDoS-as-a- Service Market
item.description: DDoS attacks are increasingly being sold like subscription services, complete with pricing tiers, support, and reseller programs. Flare explores how the DDoS-as-a-Service market has evolved from scatt...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dutch govt disrupts malware botnet with 17 million infected devices
item.description: Dutch authorities have taken offline a massive botnet of 17 million devices and seized more than 200 servers at a local provider that supported the operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
item.description: Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Chrome adds session cookie theft protection for all users
item.description: Google says the Chrome Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) security feature is now generally available and is rolling out to all users to prevent account takeovers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
item.description: Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else too, since this neo-Nazi-infested criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Russia-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
item.description:
A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025.
GREYVIBE, per W...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Man sent to prison for selling data of 7 millions elderly Americans
item.description: A North Carolina man was sentenced to more than 10 years in prison for selling the personal information of over 7 million elderly Americans to Jamaican scammers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: What 2,000 Exposed Vibe-Coded Apps Reveal About the Limits of Most Security Stacks
item.description: Shadow AI used to mean employees pasting things they shouldn't into ChatGPT. It now means something bigger: employees building full applications with AI, wiring them into production systems, and publi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US charges Google security engineer with Polymarket insider trading
item.description: A Google security engineer was charged with insider trading after winning $1.2 million using confidential company data to place bets on the cryptocurrency-based Polymarket decentralized prediction mar...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon clien...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification s...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kimsuky Deploys HTTPSpy, Expands Arsenal with HelloDoor and VS Code Tunnels
item.description: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic confirms Claude Mythos-class models will roll out to the public
item.description: Anthropic has confirmed that it plans to bring Mythos-class models to the general public after delaying the rollout due to security risks to public and private software. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
item.description: The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GreyVibe hackers use ChatGPT, Gemini to power cyberattacks
item.description: A likely Russian threat cluster tracked as GreyVibe has been targeting Ukrainian entities with AI-generated lures and a rich set of custom malware tools. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: BTMOB Android malware service generates custom phishing payloads
item.description: An Android remote access trojan named BTMOB is offered to cybercriminals with a builder interface for generating malware payloads tailored to phishing lures. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI warns of fake FIFA websites running World Cup fraud schemes
item.description: The FBI is warning of fake websites impersonating FIFA ahead of the 2026 World Cup, to steal personal and financial information, sell fake tickets and hospitality packages, and push other fraud relate...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dutch Raid Fails to Dent Russian Bulletproof Host
item.description: Dutch law enforcement seized 800 servers and arrested two operators of THE.Hosting but left the hosting provider's core IP address space intact....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hackers exploit FortiClient EMS flaw to push infostealer malware
item.description: Hackers are exploiting an authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) to deliver an undocumented credential stealer called EKZ. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
